content: Treating a 2025 SOTA memory-as-active-learning agent as the hippocampal component of a brain-inspired Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system, where the LLM generator acts as the prefrontal cortex, can create a self-improving RAG that actively seeks out missing knowledge to update its memory store instead of passively retrieving existing documents. This aligns with biological mechanisms of prefrontal-hippocampal memory interaction, where active exploration of unmemorized gaps drives long-term learning.
source_topics: memory as active learning agent SOTA 2025
trigger_topic: memory as active learning agent SOTA 2025
